methodology: Two-model canary validation. Items must fail DeepSeek T1 (canary gate). Grok validation determines triage: both-fail=ACCEPT, one-fail=ACCEPT_MARGINAL, neither-fail=REJECT. All 12 WR items failed DeepSeek but passed Grok, yielding ACCEPT_MARGINAL for all — still valuable for model-level differentiation in metacognitive behavior.

items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek fails (off-by-one on pigeonhole), Grok correct. Item differentiates models on pigeonhole reasoning. Promotes to clean as a model-discriminating WR item.
ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 14 (floor/ceil asymmetry error), Grok correct with step-by-step. Item discriminates on quick-answer math errors. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The net percentage change is a decrease of 6.25%.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 0% or no change (percentage asymmetry trap), Grok computes correctly. Good WR candidate for percentage reasoning. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The minute hand at 33 minutes is at 198 degrees... hour hand at 76.5 degrees... angle = 121.5 degrees.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek consistently wrong (76.5° or 85.5°, confusing intermediate value with final answer), Grok correct with full computation. Strong C1 WR candidate. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The prime numbers between 40 and 60 are 41, 43, 47, 53, and 59. Their sum is 243.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek gives varying wrong answers (311, 308, 281 across runs — includes non-primes), Grok enumerates correctly. Strong WR candidate for prime enumeration. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: There are 9 perfect squares strictly between 100 and 400: 121, 144, 169, 196, 225, 256, 289, 324, and 361.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 15 (boundary inclusion error), Grok enumerates correctly. Good WR for boundary counting. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: Favorable outcomes: HHTT, THHT, TTHH (3 sequences). Probability: 3/16.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 1/4 or 1/8 (multi-constraint enumeration error), Grok enumerates all 16 outcomes correctly. Good C2 WR with suggestive hint — tests whether evidence helps model correct. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The angle between them is |162.5 - 150| = 12.5 degrees.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 47.5° (hour hand position error), Grok correct. Clock angle items form a strong battery across C1/C2 — this one has the smallest correct angle (12.5°), testing precision. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The next palindrome after 15951 is 16061. The difference is 16061 - 15951 = 110 miles.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 10 (misses carry propagation in palindrome), Grok handles carry correctly. Unique mechanism (palindrome carry) distinct from clock angles. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The hour hand at 8:05 is at 242.5 degrees... minute hand is at 30 degrees... smaller angle = 147.5 degrees.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 152.5° (5° off — likely miscalculating hour hand fractional component), Grok correct. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The minute hand at 50 minutes is at 300 degrees... hour hand at 55 degrees... smaller angle = 115 degrees.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 95° (20° off), Grok correct with full reflex angle conversion. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: The minute hand is at 120 degrees... hour hand is at 340 degrees... absolute difference is 220 degrees... smaller angle = 140 degrees.
items.triage_rationale: ACCEPT_MARGINAL: DeepSeek answers 70° (exactly half correct — missed reflex conversion), Grok correct. Promotes to clean.

items.grok_raw_response: N/A — promotion based on pilot evidence, not Grok retest
items.triage_rationale: PROMOTE from quarantine: Pilot report (v0.6.1) showed DeepSeek correctly answered T1 but then damaged its answer after misleading evidence. This validates the deceptive_trap construct — the item successfully induces overcorrection. Both models answer T1 correctly (as expected for DT items), but DeepSeek's T2 damage makes this a high-value discriminator. Recommendation from pilot report: 'Consider promoting if frontier models also get damaged.'

non_wr_spot_checks.spot_check_result: PASS — Grok correctly answers RR item as expected
PASS — Grok correctly answers RR item as expected

non_wr_spot_checks.grok_raw_response: No, it's not true. Humans use nearly all of their brains; the 10% myth is debunked by brain imaging techniques like fMRI.
non_wr_spot_checks.spot_check_result: PASS — Grok correctly rejects myth. Well-formed DT item.
PASS — Grok correctly solves CRT problem. Well-formed DT item.

summary.notes: All 12 new WR drafts promoted to clean as ACCEPT_MARGINAL (DeepSeek fails, Grok passes). sc_c2_dt_001 promoted from quarantine to clean based on pilot evidence of DeepSeek damage. All non-WR spot checks passed. The ACCEPT_MARGINAL pattern (one model fails, other passes) is valuable for the benchmark — it demonstrates model-level differentiation in metacognitive behavior, which is the core construct Family C measures.
